meaning: n.冗长的官样文章,冗繁而难解的文字或语言
examples: "Gobbledegook" sounds like the name of a monster in a fairy tale.
Even so, such gobbledegook is hardly going to make life easier formotorists thinking of buying a plug-in.
Mr Wolfe's punctuation may be idiosynchratic, but his ear for corporate gobbledegook is acute.
But what he really needed was an app that could translate the patent gobbledegook contained in that Lodsys document.
